summary: Matched six functions in the item fire source by replacing placeholder/empty implementations with typed Item_GObj-based logic, adding needed effect and stage includes, and tightening header prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete signatures. The decomp.dev report recorded +772 matched bytes and raised matched code by +0.02%, with all six targeted functions reaching 100%.
what_changed: Implemented it_2725_Logic6_Spawned: gets Item via GET_ITEM, reads itFireAttributes from xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes, resets facing_dir and xDAC_itcmd_var0, calls it_802CC944, then starts behavior using it_80279CDC with attrs->x0.
Changed it_802CC740 from an empty untyped stub to void it_802CC740(Item_GObj* gobj).
Implemented itFire_UnkMotion1_Anim to call it_80272C6C and transition through it_802CC7D8 when the check fails, then return false.

Implemented it_802CC7D8 to start effect/action 0x463 through it_8027ADEC, set y velocity from attrs->x8, change item state with Item_80268E5C(gobj, 2, 2), and assign efLib_PauseAll/efLib_ResumeAll hitlag callbacks.
Added a static helper itFire_UnkMotion2_Anim_inline containing the shared setup sequence used by motion 2 animation when it_80272C6C fails.
Implemented itFire_UnkMotion2_Anim to reuse the inline helper and return true only when ip->pos.y exceeds Stage_GetBlastZoneTopOffset().
Implemented it_802CC944 to call it_802762BC, set state with Item_80268E5C(gobj, 0, 2), assign effect hitlag callbacks, and call it_80273670(gobj, 0, 0.0f).
Implemented itFire_UnkMotion0_Phys using it_8027A09C gate, effect/action 0x464, it_80273454, y velocity from attrs->x4, a static helper for state/hitlag setup, and xDD1_flag.b1 = 1.
Simplified itFire_UnkMotion0_Coll callback call by passing it_802CC740 directly to it_8027A118 instead of casting to void (*)(HSD_GObj*).
Updated itfire.h prototypes for it_802CC740, it_802CC7D8, and it_802CC944 from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete void Item_GObj* signatures.
smart_moves: Used concrete Item_GObj* prototypes in the header and source once usage was known, reducing reliance on UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS and avoiding an explicit callback cast in itFire_UnkMotion0_Coll.

Factored repeated motion setup into static inline-style helpers for itFire_UnkMotion2_Anim and itFire_UnkMotion0_Phys, suggesting the match required controlling code shape while preserving shared behavior.
Added only the includes needed by the new implementation: ef/eflib.h for efLib_PauseAll/efLib_ResumeAll, gr/stage.h for Stage_GetBlastZoneTopOffset, and it/item.h for item APIs.
Preserved item behavior idioms: GET_ITEM access, specialAttributes lookup through xC4_article_data, Item_80268E5C state changes, and hitlag callback assignment after effect/state setup.

Kept small no-op functions such as it_802CC740 and itFire_UnkMotion1_Phys as explicit empty typed functions rather than leaving placeholders, clarifying ABI/signature information without over-naming behavior.
decomp_lessons: For item articles, special attributes are commonly reached as ip->xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes and should be typed to the item-specific attributes struct when matching behavior functions.

Effect-driven item states may need both item-state transitions and effect hitlag callbacks; in this PR the repeated pattern is Item_80268E5C followed by ip->entered_hitlag = efLib_PauseAll and ip->exited_hitlag = efLib_ResumeAll.
When a collision callback API accepts a compatible typed function, fixing the callee prototype can remove forced HSD_GObj callback casts and improve header accuracy.
Stage boundary checks can be part of item animation termination; here motion 2 returns true when the item position exceeds Stage_GetBlastZoneTopOffset().
Small static helper functions can be useful matching tactics when the same source-level operation sequence appears in multiple places but the compiler requires a particular call/inline shape.
naming_conventions: Item object parameters are named gobj and use the project type Item_GObj*.
The local Item pointer is named ip and obtained with GET_ITEM(gobj).
Item-specific attribute pointers are named attrs and typed as itFireAttributes*.
Unknown item fire motions retain the existing itFire_UnkMotionN_Anim/Phys/Coll naming pattern until semantics are better understood.
Address-based symbols such as it_802CC7D8 and it_802CC944 are kept when no stronger semantic name is evidenced.
Static helper names in this PR append _inline, e.g. itFire_UnkMotion2_Anim_inline and itFire_UnkMotion0_Phys_inline, to reflect matching-oriented factoring.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduced PAD_STACK(8) in it_802CC7D8, indicating stack padding was needed for the matched code shape.
Used float literals 1.1f and 0.0f and effect/action IDs 0x463 and 0x464 exactly in the source-level reconstruction.
Factored repeated code into static helper functions to influence generated code for motion setup paths.
Changed unknown prototypes to concrete void Item_GObj* signatures to allow cleaner calls and remove a function-pointer cast.

The decomp.dev bot verified six new matches in main/melee/it/items/itfire: itFire_UnkMotion2_Anim (+188), itFire_UnkMotion0_Phys (+173), it_802CC7D8 (+136), it_802CC944 (+116), it_2725_Logic6_Spawned (+92), and itFire_UnkMotion1_Anim (+64).
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The only recorded comment was the decomp.dev bot report showing +772 matched bytes and six functions reaching 100%.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename itFire_UnkMotion0/1/2 functions or identify the semantics of effect IDs 0x463 and 0x464.
Search for other item files using the same Item_80268E5C plus efLib_PauseAll/efLib_ResumeAll pattern to reuse matching structure.
Check whether it_802CC740 remains a no-op callback or later gains a semantic name/prototype refinement.
Compare other item motion animation functions that terminate based on Stage_GetBlastZoneTopOffset for naming and behavior conventions.
